records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.726 - It is narrated that prayer should not be performed in al-Bayda', Dhat al-Salasil, the valley of al-Shaqra, or the valley of Dajnan.
records.arabicText: 726 - وَ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّهُ لاَ يُصَلَّى فِي اَلْبَيْدَاءِ وَ لاَ ذَاتِ اَلصَّلاَصِلِ وَ لاَ فِي وَادِي اَلشُّقْرَةِ وَ لاَ فِي وَادِي ضَجْنَانَ».

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.727 - Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) about praying in the bathhouse.
Imam (as) said: "If the place is clean, then there is no harm in praying, referring specifically to the changing area (maslakh).”
records.arabicText: 727 - وَ سَأَلَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ أَخَاهُ مُوسَى بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلصَّلاَةِ فِي بَيْتِ اَلْحَمَّامِ فَقَالَ «إِذَا كَانَ اَلْمَوْضِعُ نَظِيفاً فَلاَ بَأْسَ بِالصَّلاَةِ» يَعْنِي اَلْمَسْلَخَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.727 - Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) about praying in the bathhouse.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If the place is clean, then there is no harm in praying, referring specifically to the changing area (maslakh).”

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.728 - Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) said:
"Any path that is trodden and commonly used, whether it has a main road or not, should not be used for prayer."
He was asked: "Then where should one pray?"
Imam (as) said: "To the right or to the left (of the path)."
records.arabicText: 728 - وَقَالَ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «كُلُّ طَرِيقٍ يُوطَأُ وَ يُتَطَرَّقُ كَانَتْ فِيهِ جَادَّةٌ أَوْ لَمْ تَكُنْ لاَ يَنْبَغِي اَلصَّلاَةُ فِيهِ» قِيلَ فَأَيْنَ يُصَلَّى قَالَ «يَمْنَةً وَ يَسْرَةً.

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.729 - And Halabi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about praying in sheep pens, and Imam (as) said:
"Pray there, but do not pray in camel enclosures unless you fear for the safety of your belongings; in that case, sweep it and sprinkle it with water, then pray there."
He also said: "Prayer in a salty land (sabkha) is disliked unless it is a soft, level place where the forehead can rest properly."
records.arabicText: 729 - وَ سَأَلَ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلصَّلاَةِ فِي مَرَابِضِ اَلْغَنَمِ فَقَالَ «صَلِّ وَ لاَ تُصَلِّ فِي أَعْطَانِ اَلْإِبِلِ إِلاَّ أَنْ تَخَافَ عَلَى مَتَاعِكَ اَلضَّيْعَةَ فَاكْنُسْهُ وَ رُشَّهُ بِالْمَاءِ وَ صَلِّ فِيهِ» قَالَ «وَ كُرِهَ اَلصَّلاَةُ فِي اَلسَّبَخَةِ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ مَكَاناً لَيِّناً تَقَعُ عَلَيْهِ اَلْجَبْهَةُ مُسْتَوِيَةً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.729 - And Halabi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about praying in sheep pens, and Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Pray there, but do not pray in camel enclosures unless you fear for the safety of your belongings; in that case, sweep it and sprinkle it with water, then pray there."
He also said: "Prayer in a salty land (sabkha) is disliked unless it is a soft, level place where the forehead can rest properly."

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.730 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about praying in the houses of the Magians (Zoroastrians) if they are sprinkled with water, and He said:
"There is no harm in it."
He then mentioned that he sometimes saw someone on the road to Makkah sprinkling water on the spot where they placed their forehead before prostrating, while at other times, they did not sprinkle water if they deemed the place to be clean.
records.arabicText: 730 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلصَّلاَةِ فِي بُيُوتِ اَلْمَجُوسِ وَ هِيَ تُرَشُّ بِالْمَاءِ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ » ثُمَّ قَالَ وَ رَأَيْتُهُ فِي طَرِيقِ مَكَّةَ أَحْيَاناً يَرُشُّ مَوْضِعَ جَبْهَتِهِ ثُمَّ يَسْجُدُ عَلَيْهِ رَطْباً كَمَا هُوَ وَ رُبَّمَا لَمْ يَرُشَّ اَلْمَكَانَ اَلَّذِي يَرَى أَنَّهُ نَظِيفٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.730 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about praying in the houses of the Magians (Zoroastrians) if they are sprinkled with water, and He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"There is no harm in it."
He then mentioned that he sometimes saw someone on the road to Makkah sprinkling water on the spot where they placed their forehead before prostrating, while at other times, they did not sprinkle water if they deemed the place to be clean.

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.731 - Saleh ibn Al-Hakam reported that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about praying in synagogues and churches.
Imam (as) said: "Pray in them."
Salih then asked: "Even if they (non-Muslims) are praying there?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes. Do you not recite the Qur'an: 'Say, each acts according to their manner, but your Lord (azj) knows best who is rightly guided' (Surah Al-Isra, 17:84)?
Pray facing the Qibla and leave them (to their practices)."
records.arabicText:
731 - وَقَالَ صَالِحُ بْنُ اَلْحَكَمِ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلصَّلاَةِ فِي اَلْبِيَعِ وَ اَلْكَنَائِسِ فَقَالَ «صَلِّ فِيهَا» قَالَ فَقُلْتُ وَ إِنْ كَانُوا يُصَلُّونَ فِيهَا أُصَلِّي فِيهَا قَالَ «نَعَمْ أَ مَا تَقْرَأُ اَلْقُرْآنَ:
«۝ قُلْ كُلٌّ يَعْمَلُ عَلىٰ شٰاكِلَتِهِ فَرَبُّكُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنْ هُوَ أَهْدىٰ سَبِيلاً ۝» صَلِّ إِلَى اَلْقِبْلَةِ وَ دَعْهُمْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.731 - Saleh ibn Al-Hakam reported that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about praying in synagogues and churches.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Pray in them."
Salih then asked: "Even if they (non-Muslims) are praying there?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes. Do you not recite the Qur'an: 'Say, each acts according to their manner, but your Lord (azj) knows best who is rightly guided' (Surah Al-Isra, 17:84)?
Pray facing the Qibla and leave them (to their practices)."

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.732 – Zurarah asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about urine that is present on a rooftop or in a place where one prays.
Imam (as) said: "If the sun dries it, then you may pray on it, for it is purified”.
records.arabicText: 732 - وَ سَأَلَ زُرَارَةُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلْبَوْلِ يَكُونُ عَلَى اَلسَّطْحِ أَوْ فِي اَلْمَكَانِ اَلَّذِي يُصَلَّى فِيهِ فَقَالَ «إِذَا جَفَّفَتْهُ اَلشَّمْسُ فَصَلِّ عَلَيْهِ فَهُوَ طَاهِرٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.732 – Zurarah asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about urine that is present on a rooftop or in a place where one prays.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If the sun dries it, then you may pray on it, for it is purified”.

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.733 - Amir ibn Nuaym al-Qummi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about places where people stay that contain the urine of animals, manure, and are frequented by Jews and Christians.
He asked: "What should we do about praying in such places?"
Abu 'Abdillah replied: "Pray on your garment."
records.arabicText: 733 - وَ سَأَلَ عَامِرُ بْنُ نُعَيْمٍ اَلْقُمِّيُّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلْمَنَازِلِ اَلَّتِي يَنْزِلُهَا اَلنَّاسُ فِيهَا أَبْوَالُ اَلدَّوَابِّ وَ اَلسِّرْجِينُ وَ يَدْخُلُهَا اَلْيَهُودُ وَ اَلنَّصَارَى كَيْفَ نَصْنَعُ بِالصَّلاَةِ فِيهَا فَقَالَ «صَلِّ عَلَى ثَوْبِكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.733 - Amir ibn Nuaym al-Qummi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about places where people stay that contain the urine of animals, manure, and are frequented by Jews and Christians.
He asked:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"What should we do about praying in such places?"
Abu 'Abdillah replied: "Pray on your garment."

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.734 - Ali ibn Mahziyar asked Abu Al-Hasan the Third, Imam Ali ibn Muhammad Al-Hadi (as), about a man who finds himself in the desert (al-Bayda') when the time for an obligatory prayer comes and does not leave the desert until the prayer's time has passed.
He asked: "What should he do about the prayer, given that it is prohibited to pray in al-Bayda'?"
Imam (as) said: "He should pray there, but he should avoid the main path (road)."
records.arabicText: 734 - وَ سَأَلَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ مَهْزِيَارَ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ اَلثَّالِثَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَصِيرُ فِي اَلْبَيْدَاءِ فَتُدْرِكُهُ صَلاَةٌ فَرِيضَةٌ فَلاَ يَخْرُجُ مِنَ اَلْبَيْدَاءِ حَتَّى يَخْرُجَ وَقْتُهَا كَيْفَ يَصْنَعُ بِالصَّلاَةِ وَ قَدْ نُهِيَ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ بِالْبَيْدَاءِ فَقَالَ «يُصَلِّي فِيهَا وَ يَتَجَنَّبُ قَارِعَةَ اَلطَّرِيقِ.

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.735 - It was narrated from Imam (as) said, by Ayyub ibn Nuh that He said:
"One should move away from the main roads to the right or to the left and pray."
records.arabicText: 735 - وَ رَوَى عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَيُّوبُ بْنُ نُوحٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ - «يَتَنَحَّى عَنِ اَلْجَوَادِّ يَمْنَةً وَ يَسْرَةً وَ يُصَلِّي.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.735 - It was narrated from Imam (as) said, by Ayyub ibn Nuh that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "One should move away from the main roads to the right or to the left and pray."

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.736 - Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) about a house or room that does not receive sunlight and in which urine might be present and where one bathes after major ritual impurity (janabah).
He asked if it is permissible to pray there once it has dried?
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
Ali ibn Ja'far also asked about praying between graves?
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 736 - وَ سَأَلَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ أَخَاهُ مُوسَى بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلْبَيْتِ وَ اَلدَّارِ لاَ تُصِيبُهُمَا اَلشَّمْسُ وَ يُصِيبُهُمَا اَلْبَوْلُ وَ يُغْتَسَلُ فِيهِمَا مِنَ اَلْجَنَابَةِ أَ يُصَلَّى فِيهِمَا إِذَا جَفَّا قَالَ «نَعَمْ» قَالَ وَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلصَّلاَةِ بَيْنَ اَلْقُبُورِ هَلْ تَصْلُحُ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ.

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.737 - Ammar ibn Musa al-Sabati asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a mat made of reeds that had been wetted with impure water, and whether it is permissible to pray on it.
Imam (as) said: "If it has dried, then there is no harm in praying on it”.
records.arabicText: 737 - وَ سَأَلَ عَمَّارُ بْنُ مُوسَى اَلسَّابَاطِيُّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلْبَارِيَّةِ يُبَلُّ قَصَبُهَا بِمَاءٍ قَذِرٍ هَلْ تَجُوزُ اَلصَّلاَةُ عَلَيْهَا فَقَالَ «إِذَا جُفِّفَتْ فَلاَ بَأْسَ بِالصَّلاَةِ عَلَيْهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.737 - Ammar ibn Musa al-Sabati asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a mat made of reeds that had been wetted with impure water, and whether it is permissible to pray on it.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If it has dried, then there is no harm in praying on it”.

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.738 – Zurarah asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a cushion (shadhakunah) that may have come into contact with major ritual impurity (janabah) and whether it is permissible to pray on it while it is in a carriage (mahmil).
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in praying on it."
records.arabicText: 738 - وَ سَأَلَ زُرَارَةُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلشَّاذَكُونَةِ تَكُونُ عَلَيْهَا اَلْجَنَابَةُ أَ يُصَلَّى عَلَيْهَا فِي اَلْمَحْمِلِ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِالصَّلاَةِ عَلَيْهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.738 – Zurarah asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a cushion (shadhakunah) that may have come into contact with major ritual impurity (janabah) and whether it is permissible to pray on it while it is in a carriage (mahmil).
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in praying on it."

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.739 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that He said:
"There is no harm in praying on any images (representations) if you place them beneath you."
records.arabicText: 739 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «لاَ بَأْسَ بِأَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَى كُلِّ اَلتَّمَاثِيلِ إِذَا جَعَلْتَهَا تَحْتَكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.739 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in praying on any images (representations) if you place them beneath you."

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.740 - Laith al-Muradi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about cushions in the house that have images on them, whether they are to the right or to the left.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it as long as they are not facing the Qibla. If any of them are in front of you, facing the Qibla, then cover them and pray."
records.arabicText: 740 - وَ سَأَلَ لَيْثٌ اَلْمُرَادِيُّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلْوَسَائِدِ تَكُونُ فِي اَلْبَيْتِ فِيهَا اَلتَّمَاثِيلُ عَنْ يَمِينٍ أَوْ عَنْ شِمَالٍ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ مَا لَمْ تَكُنْ تُجَاهَ اَلْقِبْلَةِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ شَيْءٌ مِنْهَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْكَ مِمَّا يَلِي اَلْقِبْلَةَ فَغَطِّهِ وَ صَلِّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.740 - Laith al-Muradi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about cushions in the house that have images on them, whether they are to the right or to the left.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in it as long as they are not facing the Qibla. If any of them are in front of you, facing the Qibla, then cover them and pray."

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.741 – He, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), was asked about images on a carpet that have two eyes while you are praying.
Imam (as) said: "If it has one eye, then there is no harm, but if it has two eyes and you are praying, then it is not permissible."
records.arabicText: 741 - وَ سُئِلَ: عَنِ اَلتَّمَاثِيلِ تَكُونُ فِي اَلْبِسَاطِ لَهَا عَيْنَانِ وَ أَنْتَ تُصَلِّي فَقَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ لَهَا عَيْنٌ وَاحِدَةٌ فَلاَ بَأْسَ وَ إِنْ كَانَ لَهَا عَيْنَانِ وَ أَنْتَ تُصَلِّي فَلاَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.741 – He, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), was asked about images on a carpet that have two eyes while you are praying.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If it has one eye, then there is no harm, but if it has two eyes and you are praying, then it is not permissible."

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.742 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"There is no harm in praying while looking at images if they have only one eye."
records.arabicText: 742 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «لاَ بَأْسَ بِالصَّلاَةِ وَ أَنْتَ تَنْظُرُ إِلَى اَلتَّصَاوِيرِ إِذَا كَانَتْ بِعَيْنٍ وَاحِدَةٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.742 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in praying while looking at images if they have only one eye."

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.743 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Do not pray in a house where there is a dog, except if it is a hunting dog and you have closed a door between you and it, then there is no harm. Indeed, the angels do not enter a house where there is a dog, nor a house with images, nor a house with urine collected in a container."
records.arabicText: 743 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «لاَ تُصَلِّ فِي دَارٍ فِيهَا كَلْبٌ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ كَلْبَ صَيْدٍ وَ أَغْلَقْتَ دُونَهُ بَاباً فَلاَ بَأْسَ وَ إِنَّ اَلْمَلاَئِكَةَ لاَ تَدْخُلُ بَيْتاً فِيهِ كَلْبٌ وَ لاَ بَيْتاً فِيهِ تَمَاثِيلُ وَ لاَ بَيْتاً فِيهِ بَوْلٌ مَجْمُوعٌ فِي آنِيَةٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.743 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not pray in a house where there is a dog, except if it is a hunting dog and you have closed a door between you and it, then there is no harm. Indeed, the angels do not enter a house where there is a dog, nor a house with images, nor a house with urine collected in a container."

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.744 - Abu Basir narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said: "Whoever is in a place where they cannot reach the ground should make gestures (for prayer), even if they are in a remote area”.
records.arabicText: 744 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو بَصِيرٍ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ - «مَنْ كَانَ فِي مَوْضِعٍ لاَ يَقْدِرُ عَلَى اَلْأَرْضِ فَلْيُومِ إِيمَاءً وَ إِنْ كَانَ فِي أَرْضٍ مُنْقَطِعَةٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.744 - Abu Basir narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever is in a place where they cannot reach the ground should make gestures (for prayer), even if they are in a remote area”.

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.745 - Sama’a ibn Mehran asked Him about a captive taken by the polytheists who is prevented from performing prayer by his captor.
Imam (as) said: "He should make gestures (for prayer)."
records.arabicText: 745 - وَ سَأَلَهُ سَمَاعَةُ بْنُ مِهْرَانَ: عَنِ اَلْأَسِيرِ يَأْسِرُهُ اَلْمُشْرِكُونَ فَتَحْضُرُهُ اَلصَّلاَةُ فَيَمْنَعُهُ اَلَّذِي أَسَرَهُ مِنْهَا فَقَالَ «يُومِئُ إِيمَاءً.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.745 - Sama’a ibn Mehran asked Him about a captive taken by the polytheists who is prevented from performing prayer by his captor.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He should make gestures (for prayer)."

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.746 - Muawiya ibn Wahb asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man and a woman praying in the same room.
Imam (as) said: "If there is a space of about a handspan (shibr) between them, She may pray beside him alone and he alone; there is no harm."
records.arabicText: 746 - وَ سَأَلَ مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ وَهْبٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ وَ اَلْمَرْأَةِ يُصَلِّيَانِ فِي بَيْتٍ وَاحِدٍ فَقَالَ «إِذَا كَانَ بَيْنَهُمَا قَدْرُ شِبْرٍ صَلَّتْ بِحِذَاهُ وَحْدَهَا وَ هُوَ وَحْدَهُ لاَ بَأْسَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.746 - Muawiya ibn Wahb asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man and a woman praying in the same room.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If there is a space of about a handspan (shibr) between them, She may pray beside him alone and he alone; there is no harm."

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.747 - In the narration of Zurarah from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), said:
"If there is a distance between her and him that is about the length of a step or about the length of a cubit or more, then there is no harm if She prays beside him alone."
records.arabicText: 747 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِذَا كَانَ بَيْنَهَا وَ بَيْنَهُ قَدْرُ مَا يُتَخَطَّى أَوْ قَدْرُ عَظْمِ ذِرَاعٍ فَصَاعِداً فَلاَ بَأْسَ إِنْ صَلَّتْ بِحِذَاهُ وَحْدَهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.747 - In the narration of Zurarah from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If there is a distance between her and him that is about the length of a step or about the length of a cubit or more, then there is no harm if She prays beside him alone."

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.748 - Jamil narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) in which He said:
"There is no harm if a woman prays beside a man while he is praying. Indeed, The Prophet (sw) would pray while Aisha was lying down in front of him, even though She was menstruating. When he wanted to prostrate, he would touch her legs, and She would move them so that he could prostrate."
records.arabicText: 748 - وَ رَوَى جَمِيلٌ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ - «لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ اَلْمَرْأَةُ بِحِذَاءِ اَلرَّجُلِ وَ هُوَ يُصَلِّي فَإِنَّ اَلنَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ كَانَ يُصَلِّي وَ عَائِشَةُ مُضْطَجِعَةٌ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَ هِيَ حَائِضٌ وَ كَانَ إِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَسْجُدَ غَمَزَ رِجْلَيْهَا فَرَفَعَتْ رِجْلَيْهَا حَتَّى يَسْجُدَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.748 - Jamil narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) in which He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm if a woman prays beside a man while he is praying. Indeed, The Prophet (sw) would pray while Aisha was lying down in front of him, even though She was menstruating. When he wanted to prostrate, he would touch her legs, and She would move them so that he could prostrate."

records.chapter: What Garments Are Permissible for Prayer and What Garments Are Not
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.749 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that he asked him about wearing the skin of a dead animal (carrion) during prayer if it has been tanned.
Imam (as) said: "No, even if it has been tanned seventy times."
records.arabicText: 749 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ سَأَلَهُ: عَنْ جِلْدِ اَلْمَيْتَةِ يُلْبَسُ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ إِذَا دُبِغَ فَقَالَ «لاَ وَ إِنْ دُبِغَ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.749 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that he asked him about wearing the skin of a dead animal (carrion) during prayer if it has been tanned.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "No, even if it has been tanned seventy times."

records.chapter: What Garments Are Permissible for Prayer and What Garments Are Not
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.750 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the verse in which Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, said to Musa (as):
"Remove your sandals, for you are in the sacred valley of Tuwa" (Surah Taha 20:12).
Imam (as) said: "They were made from the skin of a dead donkey."
records.arabicText:
750 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنْ قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لِمُوسَى عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «۝ فَاخْلَعْ نَعْلَيْكَ إِنَّكَ بِالْوٰادِ اَلْمُقَدَّسِ طُوىً ۝»
قَالَ «كَانَتَا مِنْ جِلْدِ حِمَارٍ مَيِّتٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.750 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the verse in which Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, said to Musa (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Remove your sandals, for you are in the sacred valley of Tuwa" (Surah Taha 20:12).
Imam (as) said: "They were made from the skin of a dead donkey."
